metadata.title:
WANTS CHANCE TO


SUE HE GAVE AWAY

pages.raw_text:
c. n. Frazer, who was injured in
the Great Northern wreck at Milan,
Wash, In August, 1907, has insti-
tuted an action in the federal court
to set aside a settlement made by
him with the company Immediately
after the wreck in which he claims
that through false representations
the company induced him to settle
his claim for damages on the pay
ment of the sum of $35.


He says that the 16 persons in
jured in the wreck were placed III
one car and hurried to Spokane,
where they were held under lock
and key until the claim agent and
physician of the company had a
chance to talk victims of the
wreck out of the notion that they
were hurt. Frazer was told that
his injuries did not amount to
much and that the company was
not liable therefor and on these
representations he accepted the
proffered settlement.
